title: [IVDescriptors] Bail out on FindFirst/FindLast reductions on i1.
author: fhahn
description:
For i1, the induction either wraps in the second iteration (if unsigned) or computing ValidRange wraps (if signed), incorrectly resulting in a full range.

The former implies vectorizing a single-iteration loop, the latter would lead to incorrect vectorization as the sentinel value would be included in ValidRange. Bail out for both cases.

Fixes https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/173459.

tests.test_body:
define i1 @select_with_trunc_i1_iv(i64 %n, i64 %start) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %start, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %ctr = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %ctr.next, %loop ]
  %accum = phi i1 [ false, %entry ], [ %sel, %loop ]
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %iv, 0
  %trunc = trunc i64 %ctr to i1
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, i1 %accum, i1 %trunc
  %ctr.next = add i64 %ctr, 1
  %iv.next = add i64 %iv, 1
  %exit.cond = icmp eq i64 %n, %ctr
  br i1 %exit.cond, label %exit, label %loop

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i1 %sel
}

comments.author: Mel-Chen
comments.body:
> Hm, strange: is ConstantRange::getEmpty() or APInt::getSignedMinValue() broken?

+1, I have same question here.
If we really need to bail out based on the type, we could move that check to the beginning of RecurrenceDescriptor::isFindIVPattern.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body:
> Hm, strange: is ConstantRange::getEmpty() or APInt::getSignedMinValue() broken?

The code uses ConstantRange::getNonEmpty(), so for the case where Lower==Upper, a full range will be used. So for the i1 case where the range should be empty it is full instead.

Though I wonder whether there isn't an off by one in the range calculation? The ranges are half-open, so the range `[SignedMin, SignedMax - 1)` excludes both SignedMax *and* SignedMax-1. Probably only excluding SignedMax was intended? In that case the range should be `[SignedMin, SignedMax)`, which would also avoid the i1 problem.

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
> Though I wonder whether there isn't an off by one in the range calculation?

Thanks, you were absolutely right: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/174441.
